name: Calares del Mundo y de la Sima Natural Park Loop
name_original: Nacimiento del Río Mundo — vuelta por Villaverde de Guadalimar, Salobre y Riópar
description: This difficult 51.7-mile road cycling route features the Nacimiento del Río Mundo Waterfall and the challenging Puerto de Las Crucetas.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Villaverde De Guadalimar are set within the mountainous Sierra de Alcaraz in Albacete, Spain. This region features significant elevation changes, including challenging climbs and descents. A substantial part of the Parque Natural de los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima lies within the municipality, offering varied terrain and scenic backdrops. The area is characterized by its rugged mountains, river valleys, and natural monuments, providing a demanding environment for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Villaverde De Guadalimar The most popular road cycling route is Calares del Mundo y de la Sima Natural Park Loop, a 51.7 miles (83.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 28 minutes to complete. This difficult route features over 5,200 feet (1,600 meters) of elevation gain through the natural park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de Alcaraz and Río Mundo Headwaters Loop, a difficult 56.9 miles (91.6 km) path. This route includes over 6,600 feet (2,000 meters) of climbing, traversing the Sierra de Alcaraz and passing near the source of the Mundo River. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto de las Crucetillas – Los Infiernos Viewpoint loop from Puerto del Arenal, an 81.5 miles (131.1 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with over 7,700 feet (2,300 meters) of ascent, often completed in about 6 hours 52 minutes. Road cycling around Villaverde De Guadalimar is defined by significant elevation changes, mountainous landscapes of the Sierra de Alcaraz, and routes through the Calares del Mundo y de la Sima Natural Park. The network offers options primarily for advanced road cyclists seeking challenging ascents and descents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villaverde De Guadalimar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the typical terrain like for road cycling in Villaverde De Guadalimar?
A: Road cycling in Villaverde De Guadalimar is characterized by its mountainous environment within the Sierra de Alcaraz. You can expect significant elevation changes, including challenging climbs and descents, as routes often traverse rugged mountains and river valleys. The region is primarily suited for advanced road cyclists seeking demanding ascents.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes in Villaverde De Guadalimar?
A: The road cycling routes around Villaverde De Guadalimar are generally considered difficult due to the mountainous terrain and significant elevation gains. All 13 routes listed in this guide are classified as difficult, with some featuring over 2,000 meters of climbing. There are no easy or moderate road cycling routes detailed here.

Q: What kind of elevation gains can I expect on road cycling routes here?
A: Road cycling routes in Villaverde De Guadalimar feature substantial elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169102410/calares-del-mundo-y-de-la-sima-natural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calares del Mundo y de la Sima Natural Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; includes over 1,600 meters of ascent, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280171358/sierra-de-alcaraz-and-rio-mundo-headwaters-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Alcaraz and Río Mundo Headwaters Loop&lt;/a&gt; has over 2,000 meters of climbing. The most challenging route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20125353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de las Crucetillas – Los Infiernos Viewpoint loop from Puerto del Arenal&lt;/a&gt;, features over 2,300 meters of ascent.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while road cycling in this region?
A: The region offers breathtaking natural landmarks. You can cycle through the Parque Natural de los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima, which is home to the dramatic Nacimiento del Río Mundo waterfall. Routes may also offer views of the Pico de la Sarga and Piedra del Cambrón, both providing extensive panoramic vistas. The Arroyo del Tejo area features rock formations known as Los Picarazos, offering views of the Guadalimar valley.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in scenic viewpoints. The Chorros del Mundo Viewpoint, located on the heights of Calar del Mundo, offers sweeping panoramic views of the Mundo River valley and surrounding peaks. The Piedra del Cambrón also provides extensive panoramic views across several provinces, making for memorable stops during your ride.

Q: Can I visit any historical sites or castles during my road cycling trip?
A: While primarily known for its natural beauty, the region does have historical points of interest. You can find highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5588608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riópar Viejo&lt;/a&gt; settlement or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4577208" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Yedra Ruins&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable structures include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6536732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Keep Tower of Hornos de Segura&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5405804" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cubo Tower in Siles&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Villaverde De Guadalimar are circular. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169102410/calares-del-mundo-y-de-la-sima-natural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calares del Mundo y de la Sima Natural Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40302588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Siles – Era del Fustal Shelter loop from Cabañas Rurales&lt;/a&gt; are both designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point.

Q: Are there options for long-distance road cycling?
A: Yes, Villaverde De Guadalimar offers several long-distance road cycling options. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20125353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de las Crucetillas – Los Infiernos Viewpoint loop from Puerto del Arenal&lt;/a&gt; span over 131 kilometers, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20125316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinicos – Coimbra Del Barranco Ancho loop from Puerto del Arenal&lt;/a&gt; covers nearly 120 kilometers, providing challenging rides for experienced cyclists.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Villaverde De Guadalimar?
A: The region's mountainous character means that spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. The Nacimiento del Río Mundo waterfall is particularly spectacular during its 'bursting season,' typically in March, which can add to the scenic value of a spring ride. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder temperatures and potential for snow at higher elevations.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Villaverde De Guadalimar?
A: Road cyclists frequently praise the challenging nature of the routes and the stunning natural beauty of the Sierra de Alcaraz and the Parque Natural de los Calares del Mundo y de la Sima. The significant elevation changes and panoramic views from points like the Chorros del Mundo Viewpoint are often highlighted as key attractions. Over 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, indicating its popularity among those seeking a demanding and rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any mountain passes to conquer on a road bike in this area?
A: Absolutely. The region is home to several challenging mountain passes that are popular with road cyclists. Notable passes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5818831" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de las Crucetillas&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5822424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Bellotar&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5818918" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Las Crucetas&lt;/a&gt;. These passes contribute to the significant elevation gains found on many routes.

Q: Is public transport available to access road cycling routes in Villaverde De Guadalimar?
A: Information regarding specific public transport options directly serving the starting points of road cycling routes in Villaverde De Guadalimar is limited. Given its mountain village setting, public transport access might be infrequent or require connections. It is advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to the area, especially if bringing your own bike.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes?
A: While specific parking locations for each route are not detailed here, Villaverde De Guadalimar is a small village, and parking is generally available within or on the outskirts of the village. For routes starting from specific points like 'Puerto del Arenal', you would typically find parking facilities near these common starting locations. It's recommended to check local maps or tourist information upon arrival for the most convenient parking spots.
tips: A fairly tough mountain pass, with ramps between 7 and 10%, in total there are more than six kilometers. There is hardly any traffic during weekdays, but tourists tend to travel on weekends.

As far as the asphalt ends, to see the waterfalls from the source of the Mundo River, you have to walk a bit along the trails, there are three viewpoints in total. It is totally worth it, especially after rainy days.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult and requires very good fitness. It features significant elevation gain, including the challenging Puerto de Las Crucetas, a mountain pass with gradients between 7% and 10% over six kilometers. It's best suited for experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the spectacular Nacimiento del Río Mundo Waterfall, where the Mundo River emerges from a cave. You'll also traverse the demanding Puerto de Las Crucetas, offering rewarding views, and enjoy the stunning natural landscapes of the Natural Park of Calares del Mundo y de la Sima.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily consists of paved surfaces through mountainous terrain. Expect demanding climbs and descents, particularly on the mountain passes. The overall elevation gain is substantial, making it a challenging ride.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route can be enjoyed in various seasons, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling. After significant rainfall, you might witness the 'reventón' phenomenon at the Nacimiento del Río Mundo, a powerful burst of water from the spring, which is a spectacular sight.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, you would typically look for parking in the towns along the route, such as Riópar, Villaverde de Guadalimar, or Salobre, to begin your ride. Parking near the Nacimiento del Río Mundo Waterfall is also an option, though the asphalt road ends a short walk from the viewpoints.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or to access the Natural Park of Calares del Mundo y de la Sima. However, it's always good practice to check for any local regulations or temporary restrictions before your visit, especially concerning protected natural areas.

faqs.question: Is the Nacimiento del Río Mundo — vuelta por Villaverde de Guadalimar, Salobre y Riópar route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling. While dogs are generally allowed in the Natural Park of Calares del Mundo y de la Sima, bringing a dog on a demanding road cycling route of this nature is not typically recommended for their safety or comfort. If you plan to visit the waterfall area on foot, dogs are usually permitted on a leash.

faqs.question: Are there places to refill water or find amenities along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, public fountains are available at various points along the route, which can be useful for refilling water bottles. The towns of Villaverde de Guadalimar, Salobre, and Riópar offer amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly crosses or runs parallel to sections of other paths. Notably, it intersects with parts of the Sendero Calar del Mundo and the Sendero Casa de la Noguera-Puerto del Arenal, among others, though these are typically hiking trails.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this challenging road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should pack essentials like sufficient water and nutrition, repair kit, spare tubes, appropriate cycling attire for varying weather conditions, sun protection, and a first-aid kit. A GPS device with the route loaded is also highly recommended.

faqs.question: Is the 'reventón' phenomenon at Nacimiento del Río Mundo common?
faqs.answer: The 'reventón' is a unique and powerful burst of water from the Nacimiento del Río Mundo Waterfall. It typically occurs after heavy rainfall, especially during spring or late autumn, when the underground water system is saturated. It's not a constant occurrence but a spectacular event to witness if conditions are right.
